gaps: 🧠 LIFECYCLE DIMENSION — Post-update language display persistence: The bug shows that after a firmware update with language change, the settings screen displays the *previous* language (not the newly updated one). No existing test verifies what language value is *shown* in the Device Settings → Language selection immediately after a DFU completes. Tests 66363556-66363559 verify DFU success/failure and pop-ups, but none check the displayed language value in the settings UI post-update.

🧠 LIFECYCLE DIMENSION — 'Update currently not available' dialog on re-selection: The bug shows that attempting to change the language *again* after the update shows a blocking dialog. No existing test covers the scenario of tapping the language selection option a second time post-update to verify whether the dialog appears correctly or incorrectly.

🧠 STATE TRIGGER — Dialog trigger timing: The bug implies the dialog appears when it shouldn't (or the wrong dialog appears). No test validates the exact state transition that triggers this dialog — e.g., is it triggered by the UI detecting a pending update state that wasn't cleared, or by a mismatch between the displayed language and the actual device language?

🧠 LIFECYCLE DIMENSION — App restart behavior: The bug steps include closing and restarting the app to check firmware version. No existing test verifies that after an app kill/relaunch, the language selection UI correctly reflects the updated language from the device. This is a critical persistence gap across app lifecycle.

🧠 DEVICE ASYMMETRY — Device-specific behavior: Existing tests are heavily biased toward Voyager Free 60+ (earbuds with smart charge case). The bug scenario is generic to 'Poly Bluetooth audio device' but the existing test coverage does not include simpler devices like Poly Sync 20/20+ speakerphones or other headset form factors that lack a charge case. The language update flow may behave differently on devices without multiple firmware components (earbud + case).

🧠 STATE TRIGGER — Language value vs. firmware version mismatch: The bug steps ask to check firmware version after restart, implying the language issue may correlate with a stale firmware version display. No test validates the relationship between the displayed firmware version and the displayed language selection post-update.

generated_testcases.title: TC-LANG-001: Verify displayed language in Device Settings immediately after DFU completes (Poly Sync 20+)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on the paired mobile device.
Tap the [Sync 20+] device name from the home screen to enter Device Settings.
Navigate to Device Settings → Language selection and note the currently displayed language (e.g., 'English').
Go back to Device Settings main screen.
Initiate a firmware update via Device Settings → Firmware Update → Check for Updates.
Allow the firmware update to complete 100% and observe the 'Update successful' confirmation pop-up.
Do NOT close or restart the app. Immediately tap OK/dismiss the pop-up.
Navigate back to Device Settings → Language selection.
Observe the language value displayed in the Language selection row.
generated_testcases.expected: The Language selection row displays the newly updated language (the language selected during the firmware update process), NOT the previous language. If no language was changed during the update, the displayed language should remain the same as before the update.

generated_testcases.title: TC-LANG-002: Verify 'Update currently not available' dialog does NOT appear when tapping Language selection post-update (Poly Sync 20+)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to [Sync 20+].
Navigate to Device Settings → Language selection and note the current language.
Perform a firmware update that includes a language change (e.g., change from English to French during the update flow).
Wait for the update to complete successfully and dismiss the success pop-up.
Immediately navigate to Device Settings → Language selection and tap on it.
Observe the screen/dialog that appears.
generated_testcases.expected: The Language selection screen opens normally showing the list of available languages with the newly updated language selected. The 'Update currently not available' blocking dialog does NOT appear.

generated_testcases.title: TC-LANG-003: Verify Language selection UI reflects updated language after app kill and relaunch (Poly Sync 20+)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to [Sync 20+].
Navigate to Device Settings → Language selection and note the current language (e.g., 'English').
Perform a firmware update that changes the device language (e.g., English → German).
Wait for the update to complete successfully and dismiss the success pop-up.
Fully close (kill) the Poly Studio Mobile app from the phone's recent apps menu.
Reopen the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Wait for the app to reconnect to [Sync 20+].
Navigate to Device Settings → Language selection.
Observe the language value displayed.
generated_testcases.expected: The Language selection row displays the updated language (German) that was set during the firmware update. The app correctly reads and displays the current device language after a full app restart, not a stale/cached value.

generated_testcases.title: TC-LANG-004: Verify firmware version displayed post-update matches updated language state (Poly Sync 20+)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to [Sync 20+].
Navigate to Device Settings → About/Device Info and note the current firmware version (e.g., v1.2.3).
Navigate to Device Settings → Language selection and note the current language (e.g., 'English').
Perform a firmware update that updates both the firmware version (e.g., to v1.2.4) and changes the language (e.g., English → Spanish).
Wait for the update to complete successfully and dismiss the success pop-up.
Navigate to Device Settings → About/Device Info and note the new firmware version.
Navigate to Device Settings → Language selection and observe the displayed language.
Fully kill and relaunch the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Reconnect to [Sync 20+] and navigate to Device Settings → About/Device Info and Language selection.
Compare the firmware version and language values before and after the restart.
generated_testcases.expected: Both the firmware version (v1.2.4) and the language (Spanish) are correctly displayed immediately after the update AND persist correctly after app kill/relaunch. There is no mismatch where the firmware version updates but the language remains stale, or vice versa.

generated_testcases.title: TC-LANG-005: Verify Language selection behavior on device without charge case (Poly Sync 20) — post-update display
steps: Connect to a [Poly Sync 20] (non-Plus variant, no smart charge case) via the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to Device Settings → Language selection and note the current language (e.g., 'English').
Initiate a firmware update via Device Settings → Firmware Update → Check for Updates, selecting a different language (e.g., change to French).
Allow the firmware update to complete 100% and dismiss the success pop-up.
Navigate to Device Settings → Language selection and observe the displayed language.
Kill and relaunch the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Reconnect to [Sync 20] and navigate back to Device Settings → Language selection.
Observe the displayed language again.
generated_testcases.expected: On Poly Sync 20 (no charge case), the Language selection correctly displays the updated language (French) both immediately after the update and after app kill/relaunch. The behavior is consistent with earbud devices — the absence of a charge case does not cause the language to revert or display incorrectly.

generated_testcases.title: TC-LANG-006: Verify tapping Language selection a second time post-update does not show stale dialog (Poly Sync 20+)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to [Sync 20+].
Navigate to Device Settings → Language selection and note the current language.
Perform a firmware update that changes the language (e.g., English → Italian).
Wait for the update to complete successfully and dismiss the success pop-up.
Navigate to Device Settings → Language selection and tap to open it. Verify the language list opens normally.
Close the language selection screen (go back to Device Settings).
Immediately tap Language selection again to open it a second time.
Observe the screen that appears.
generated_testcases.expected: The Language selection screen opens normally on the second tap as well. No 'Update currently not available' dialog or any other blocking dialog appears. The newly updated language (Italian) is shown as selected in the list.

generated_testcases.title: TC-LANG-007: Verify language display after Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect post-update (Poly Sync 20+)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to [Sync 20+].
Navigate to Device Settings → Language selection and note the current language (e.g., 'English').
Perform a firmware update that changes the language (e.g., English → Japanese).
Wait for the update to complete successfully and dismiss the success pop-up.
Navigate to Device Settings → Language selection and verify the updated language is displayed.
Disconnect Bluetooth from the phone's Bluetooth settings or turn off the Sync 20+.
Wait 10 seconds, then reconnect Bluetooth (turn Sync 20+ back on or re-pair).
Open Poly Studio Mobile app and wait for reconnection.
Navigate to Device Settings → Language selection.
Observe the displayed language.
generated_testcases.expected: The Language selection row displays the updated language (Japanese) after Bluetooth disconnect and reconnect. The language value is correctly re-read from the device and not cached from a previous session.

generated_testcases.title: TC-LANG-008: Verify language selection UI when NO language change is made during firmware update (Poly Sync 20+)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to [Sync 20+].
Navigate to Device Settings → Language selection and note the current language (e.g., 'English').
Initiate a firmware update via Device Settings → Firmware Update → Check for Updates.
During the update flow, do NOT change the language — keep the default/current language selected.
Allow the firmware update to complete 100% and dismiss the success pop-up.
Navigate to Device Settings → Language selection and observe the displayed language.
Kill and relaunch the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Reconnect to [Sync 20+] and navigate back to Device Settings → Language selection.
Observe the displayed language again.
generated_testcases.expected: The Language selection row displays the same language (English) that was set before the update, both immediately after the update and after app kill/relaunch. No language regression or unexpected change occurs when the language is not explicitly changed during the update.
